summary: We are in full execution mode for the China Going Global report launch in September and the Intelligent China program launch, with urgent SEO prioritization and social media agency selection this week.
summaryDetail: The top priority is the China Going Global report with IE University, targeting a September launch at Tech in Asia or Tyson events in Singapore. Key actions this week: Felix must finalize the one-pager proposal and meet with Tech in Asia/Tyson; Julie needs to identify op-ed contributors and case studies; the team begins translation. Simultaneously, we are prioritizing the Intelligent China program and Asia Star over US partnerships, with Joseph tasked to get the Intelligent China website ready and the team deciding on campaign approach. Urgently this week: expedite social media agency selection—all four agencies are invited, with RFP and target audience profile due by July 6; Joseph must provide brief on conversion tracking and draft SEO target areas. Also pressing: finalize Shun Tian Shu press release (Jimmy incorporates 10 points), update Ding Hight Hall video, and publish Asia Start spotlight articles. Upcoming next week: publish Forbes CEOs press release (July 6), start work on report parts (July 7), discuss Professor Zhu's new book plans, and coordinate Dean Xiang's QS Bali keynote.

meetings.title: SEO & Program Prioritization Strategy
meetings.summary: Team discusses SEO strategy to rank for programs in China (AI, EVs, smart manufacturing) and prioritizes Asia Star and Intelligent China programs over US partnerships.

meetings.title: Marketing Team Meeting - June 29, 2026
meetings.summary: Team discussed website menu feedback, brochure updates, CES quotes, Ding Hight Hall video, Asia Start articles, Davos press release, social media agency scouting, and IE report translation. Decisions made on Shun Tian Shu press release approach and agency prioritization.

meetings.title: New Chinese Playbook Report Planning
meetings.summary: Team discusses joint report with CKGSB on Chinese companies globalizing to Southeast Asia, including op-eds, case studies, and launch at Tech in Asia or Tyson event in Singapore in September.

meetings.title: Asia Start & New Program Planning
meetings.summary: Reviewed campaign results for Asia Start, discussed two routes (AI vs traditional) for November campaign, and introduced a new in-depth program 'Intelligent China' targeting foreigners with modules in Beijing, Shanghai/Hangzhou, and Shenzhen.

meetings.title: Europe-China Cooperation Discussion
meetings.summary: Discussion on geopolitical tensions affecting European companies in China, with emphasis on de-risking and the role of companies versus governments.

decisions.text: Prioritize Asia Star and Intelligent China programs; US partnerships (Stanford, Columbia, Berkeley) secondary.
Focus on outcomes and target audience understanding for SEO strategy.
Only one press release for Shun Tian Shu, either incorporating 10 points or turning into 10-point article.
Prioritize speed in social media agency selection; likely stick with Media Minds due to small budget.
Report will be jointly published by CKGSB and IE, titled 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global'.
Report will include main research from professor Leeway, op-eds from both schools' leadership, and industry case studies.
Target completion by early September for launch at Singapore events in mid-September.
Asia Start November campaign will test two routes: AI (Joseph) and traditional (Daisy) for the first month, then decide on one.
Invite all four agencies (Impactable, Media Minds Global, 香港麦炽科技有限公司, 省广) to participate in the social media ad agency selection.
decisions.meetingTitle: Re: Request for Proposal - social media ad agency 2026 - updated 0703

decisions.text: Use Flow for design of China Going Global report due to flexibility and quality.
Proceed with joint report with IE University on China's New Playbook for Going Global, planned for September 2026.
decisions.meetingTitle: 2026年英文报告:  China’s New Playbook for Going Global
